verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type-I single-pass transmembrane protein with a substantial extracellular/ectodomain and a bona fide TM anchor. However, its expression is strictly sperm-specific (germline/reproductive lineage), and crucially, surface display on the outer leaflet occurs only after the acrosome reaction — prior to which it resides on the inner acrosomal membrane and is inaccessible to extracellular antibodies. The outer-leaflet pool is real and supported by live-cell surface biotinylation/immunofluorescence data post-acrosome reaction, but it is restricted to mature acrosome-reacted sperm. Per triage rules, germline/gamete-restricted surface display with its own anchor maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface, not yes.
